tasks.title: Define the weapon runtime profile
tasks.summary: Define immutable validated four-mount runtime tuning without defining weapon content packages.
tasks.objective: Represent cadence, burst timing, heat or charge, recovery, power-bank cost rules, recoil influence, behavior-module references, and presentation priority in one deterministic engine-independent profile.
tasks.context: S1.2 frozen question: can one shared aim/fire intent drive four independently simulated mounts with unlimited normal fire, independent power banks, bounded movement influence, readable state, and modular behavior inside the accepted ten-day cap?
inputs: Consumed contracts: StableId v1 from CS-001, BuildIdentity/ContentVersion v1 from CS-002, Combat Messages v1 from CS-004, and Four-Mount Weapons v1 from CS-005.
Accepted S1.2 combat boundaries from ARCHITECTURE.md and REQUIREMENTS.md.
outputs: Profile validation and deterministic identity rules
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: accepted planning/requirements, movement-owned files, UnityAdapters, ContentPackages/Weapons, generated registries, scenes, prefabs, ScriptableObjects, UI, enemies, mission state, saves, rewards, and networking.
Serialized ownership: none. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none; behavior references use validated stable IDs without generating content.
Keep this a runtime profile, not a universal weapon god object and not a replacement for the five amended Stage 1 weapon packages.
Rollback: remove the three owned files before any consumer lands. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: Profiles are immutable, Unity-free, deterministically identifiable, and reject invalid cadence, cost, heat/charge, recovery, recoil-influence, module-reference, and priority values.
Normal fire has no consumable ammunition field; every mount profile has an independent optional power bank and authored empowered cost.
The model does not encode the five amended Stage 1 weapon packages or switch on weapon IDs.
verification: Automated: deterministic identity, invalid numeric bounds, missing module reference, unlimited-normal-fire, and independent-power-field tests.
Manual/playable: contract review confirms all five amended Stage 1 weapon packages can be represented without adding package-specific branches.
proof_required: representative valid and rejected profile fixtures
tasks.handoff_notes: CB-002 through CB-006 consume this profile; WP tasks own actual weapon packages and tuning values.

tasks.title: Implement the independent weapon-mount state machine
tasks.summary: Define deterministic cadence, burst, heat/charge, recovery, readiness, and fault transitions for one mount.
tasks.objective: Ensure every mounted weapon advances independently so one cooldown, charge, recovery, or fault cannot stall another mount.
tasks.context: S1.2 frozen question: can one shared aim/fire intent drive four independently simulated mounts with unlimited normal fire, independent power banks, bounded movement influence, readable state, and modular behavior inside the accepted ten-day cap?
inputs: Consumed contract: Four-Mount Weapons v1 from CS-005.
Validated WeaponRuntimeProfile from CB-001.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: accepted planning/requirements, movement-owned files, UnityAdapters, ContentPackages, generated registries, scenes, prefabs, UI, mission state, and saves.
Serialized ownership: none. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none.
The stepper operates on one mount only and may not coordinate, wait for, or mutate another mount.
Rollback: remove the three owned files and retain CS-005 contracts. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: Cadence, burst, heat/charge, recovery, readiness, and fault transitions are deterministic for a fixed profile and fixed-step sequence.
A mount can be ready, firing, recovering, depleted, or faulted without changing another mount's state.
Invalid elapsed time, impossible transition, or malformed state fails closed with actionable diagnostics.
verification: Automated: mixed cadence, burst interruption, overheat/charge completion, recovery, rapid input, malformed state, and isolated fault tests.
Manual/playable: inspect four synthetic mount traces with deliberately different cadence and recovery values.
tasks.handoff_notes: CB-006 owns four-mount coordination; do not add cross-mount state here.

tasks.title: Implement independent power-bank and fallback rules
tasks.summary: Define deterministic per-mount power expenditure, authored refill eligibility, and normal-fire fallback.
tasks.objective: Keep every power-ammo bank independent so depletion falls back immediately for that mount while other ready mounts remain empowered.
tasks.context: S1.2 frozen question: can one shared aim/fire intent drive four independently simulated mounts with unlimited normal fire, independent power banks, bounded movement influence, readable state, and modular behavior inside the accepted ten-day cap?
inputs: Consumed contract: Four-Mount Weapons v1 from CS-005.
Validated empowered cost and bank rules from CB-001.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: accepted planning/requirements, economy, rewards, pickups, shops, movement, UnityAdapters, ContentPackages, registries, scenes, UI, and saves.
Serialized ownership: none. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none.
No passive power-bank regeneration is introduced. This task supports expenditure and explicit authored refill eligibility only; later content/economy tasks own refill sources.
Rollback: remove the three owned files and retain CS-005 contracts. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: Power expenditure is atomic per mount and cannot consume another mount's bank.
Insufficient power produces normal-fire fallback for that mount without suppressing other empowered mounts.
Refill commands are explicitly validated and no passive regeneration occurs.
verification: Automated: exact-cost, insufficient-cost, overfill, negative refill, simultaneous mixed-bank, and repeated fallback tests.
Manual/playable: review a four-bank trace where two mounts empower, one falls back, and one is not ready.
tasks.handoff_notes: Later refill-source tasks issue validated refill commands; they do not bypass this policy.

tasks.title: Define the modular weapon-behavior pipeline
tasks.summary: Define an engine-independent module boundary and deterministic fire execution plan without implementing weapon packages.
tasks.objective: Allow compact reusable automatic, projectile, spread/burst, beam/heat, charge/pierce, homing, chain, and lobbed-area modules without a global weapon switch statement.
tasks.context: S1.2 frozen question: can one shared aim/fire intent drive four independently simulated mounts with unlimited normal fire, independent power banks, bounded movement influence, readable state, and modular behavior inside the accepted ten-day cap?
inputs: Consumed contracts: Combat Messages v1 from CS-004 and Four-Mount Weapons v1 from CS-005.
Validated module references from CB-001.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: accepted planning/requirements, UnityAdapters, ContentPackages/Weapons, generated registries, scenes, prefabs, ScriptableObjects, concrete weapon modules, enemies, movement, UI, and saves.
Serialized ownership: none. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: consumes stable module IDs only and generates nothing.
Define only the minimal reusable boundary and plan primitives accepted by the architecture; do not build generalized authoring tools or implement any named weapon.
Rollback: remove the four owned files before package modules consume them. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: A module receives immutable validated input and returns a deterministic execution plan without Unity types or side effects.
Pipeline order and result composition are stable, bounded, and reject unknown/duplicate module IDs.
Adding an isolated test module requires no edit to a universal weapon switch statement.
verification: Automated: module ordering, duplicate/unknown module, bounded-plan, deterministic replay, and fault-isolation tests using synthetic modules.
Manual/playable: architecture review maps the accepted behavior families to the same boundary without implementing them.
tasks.handoff_notes: WP tasks implement only the modules required by their owned packages; ordinary content never edits this pipeline.

tasks.title: Resolve shared aim for four mount origins
tasks.summary: Resolve one aim intent and world point into four stable per-mount firing solutions.
tasks.objective: Preserve one player aim point while accounting for independent mount origins, close-range convergence safeguards, minimum direction, and stable slot ordering.
tasks.context: S1.2 frozen question: can one shared aim/fire intent drive four independently simulated mounts with unlimited normal fire, independent power banks, bounded movement influence, readable state, and modular behavior inside the accepted ten-day cap?
inputs: Consumed contracts: Player Intents v1 from CS-003 and stable four-slot ordering from CS-005.
Validated mount geometry safeguards from CB-001.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: accepted planning/requirements, UnityAdapters, camera, input assets, movement, ContentPackages, scenes, prefabs, UI, registries, and saves.
Serialized ownership: none. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none.
Use engine-independent vector/value types from accepted contracts; do not read cursor, camera, Transform, or device state.
Rollback: remove the three owned files and retain CS-003/CS-005 contracts. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: Exactly four solutions preserve stable slot order and converge on one aim point when geometry allows.
Aim points at/behind/inside mount geometry produce bounded deterministic fallback directions without NaN or zero vectors.
Resolving one mount never changes another mount's origin or state.
verification: Automated: distant aim, close aim, aim inside geometry, coincident origin, behind-player aim, mirrored origins, and stable-order tests.
Manual/playable: human review of plotted four-ray solutions confirms intuitive convergence and no cross-over instability.
tasks.handoff_notes: CB-008 owns device-to-intent conversion; CB-006 consumes only resolved domain solutions.

tasks.title: Implement the four-mount combat coordinator
tasks.summary: Apply one aim, fire, and power intent to exactly four independent mount simulations.
tasks.objective: Combine readiness, per-mount power fallback, behavior planning, and fire results without synchronizing cadence or allowing one fault to suppress another mount.
tasks.context: S1.2 frozen question: can one shared aim/fire intent drive four independently simulated mounts with unlimited normal fire, independent power banks, bounded movement influence, readable state, and modular behavior inside the accepted ten-day cap?
inputs: Consumed contracts: Player Intents v1, Combat Messages v1, and Four-Mount Weapons v1.
Per-mount stepper, power policy, behavior pipeline, and shared-aim resolver from CB-002 through CB-005.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: accepted planning/requirements, UnityAdapters, movement-owned files, ContentPackages, scenes, prefabs, UI, registries, enemies, mission state, rewards, and saves.
Serialized ownership: none. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: consumes validated runtime profiles only.
Use fixed stable slot ordering and isolate per-mount faults. Do not introduce a universal reload, shared cooldown, shared ammo pool, or synchronized fire gate.
Rollback: remove the three owned files and keep lower-level domain components independently testable. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: One fire intent advances all and only ready mounts while preserving each mount's independent cadence and recovery.
Power intent empowers each eligible mount independently and falls back per depleted mount without blocking normal fire.
One mount fault is recorded but does not suppress the other three mount results.
verification: Automated: mixed cadence, simultaneous fire, mixed readiness, mixed power, one/two faults, rapid intent, stable ordering, and deterministic replay tests.
Manual/playable: inspect a four-lane timeline proving independent cadence and per-mount fallback under one input stream.
tasks.handoff_notes: Unity adapters consume immutable execution results; they may not become combat-state authorities.

tasks.title: Bridge recoil and firing influence into movement
tasks.summary: Apply validated combat movement influence through an explicit adapter without creating another velocity authority.
tasks.objective: Translate bounded recoil and authored heavy-weapon movement influence into the accepted movement runtime while preserving thruster and collision authority.
tasks.context: S1.2 frozen question: can one shared aim/fire intent drive four independently simulated mounts with unlimited normal fire, independent power banks, bounded movement influence, readable state, and modular behavior inside the accepted ten-day cap?
inputs: Movement body/application boundaries from MT-008 and MT-010.
Validated recoil/movement influence and immutable combat results from CB-001/CB-006.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: accepted planning/requirements, all movement-owned files, Rigidbody2D authority, thruster policies, ContentPackages, scenes, prefabs, UI, registries, and saves.
Serialized ownership: none. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none.
The adapter submits bounded influence through the movement-owned boundary and may not set velocity directly, edit MT files, bypass wall/contact rules, or accumulate an unbounded penalty.
Rollback: remove the three owned files; movement remains independently playable. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: Combat influence is capped, deterministic, and enters through one explicit movement boundary.
No combat file writes Rigidbody2D velocity or mutates movement/thruster state directly.
Combined heavy-weapon effects preserve accepted movement and thruster invariants.
verification: Automated: zero/one/four influence, cap, opposite recoil, simultaneous fire, thruster-active, wall-contact, and adapter-removal tests.
Manual/playable: human review compares no-fire and synthetic four-mount recoil traces for responsiveness and bounded influence.
tasks.handoff_notes: This is the sole combat-to-movement bridge and requires stronger review because it crosses subsystem boundaries.

tasks.title: Create the combat input action map and adapter
tasks.summary: Map keyboard, mouse, and gamepad combat actions into device-independent player intent contracts.
tasks.objective: Produce shared aim, fire, and power intents with consistent held/pressed/released, focus-loss, and disconnect behavior without device checks in combat rules.
tasks.context: S1.2 frozen question: can one shared aim/fire intent drive four independently simulated mounts with unlimited normal fire, independent power banks, bounded movement influence, readable state, and modular behavior inside the accepted ten-day cap?
inputs: Pinned Input System/assemblies from UF-002/UF-004.
Player Intents v1 from CS-003, evidence fixture rules from EH-002, and device lifecycle conventions from MT-007.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: accepted planning/requirements, ShooterMoverMovement.inputactions, movement adapters, domain combat rules, ContentPackages, scenes, prefabs, UI, registries, and saves.
Serialized ownership: exclusive ownership of ShooterMoverCombat.inputactions. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none.
Reuse MT-007 focus-loss, disconnect, device-switch, and session-lifecycle conventions. The separate combat asset must compose without becoming a second input-session authority.
Rollback: remove the action asset, adapter, and test; movement input remains intact. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: Keyboard/mouse and gamepad produce equivalent device-independent aim, fire, and power intents.
Focus loss, disconnect, disable, and device switch clear held fire/power state deterministically using MT-007 lifecycle conventions.
The combat action asset composes with the movement action asset without duplicate callbacks or conflicting session ownership.
verification: Automated: held/pressed/released, focus-loss, disconnect, device-switch, simultaneous movement/combat maps, and duplicate-callback tests.
Manual/playable: human review verifies keyboard/mouse and gamepad aim/fire parity using synthetic intent displays.
tasks.handoff_notes: This task owns only the combat action asset; shared input-session behavior remains aligned with MT-007.

tasks.title: Bridge combat execution plans to Unity 2D
tasks.summary: Translate validated engine-independent fire plans and confirmed 2D hits through explicit Unity adapters.
tasks.objective: Provide 2D mount and hit-facing adapter boundaries without adding concrete weapon prefabs, enemy implementations, three-dimensional physics, or combat authority in scenes.
tasks.context: S1.2 frozen question: can one shared aim/fire intent drive four independently simulated mounts with unlimited normal fire, independent power banks, bounded movement influence, readable state, and modular behavior inside the accepted ten-day cap?
inputs: 2D-only baseline/assemblies from UF-003/UF-004.
Combat Messages v1, execution plans, and immutable coordinator results from CS-004/CB-004/CB-006.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: accepted planning/requirements, Physics3D APIs, movement-owned files, ContentPackages/Weapons, generated registries, scenes, prefabs, ScriptableObjects, enemies, UI, and saves.
Serialized ownership: none. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: consumes validated runtime plans only.
Adapters apply plans and emit confirmed CS-004 messages; they never own mount state, damage authority, weapon identity, pooling policy, or durable truth.
Rollback: remove the three owned files; domain combat remains fully testable. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: Only 2D Unity APIs are used and all applied work originates in a validated execution plan.
Confirmed 2D hits translate once into immutable CS-004 messages with stable source/target identity.
No weapon prefab, concrete projectile content, enemy behavior, or scene-owned combat state is introduced.
verification: Automated: plan ordering, invalid plan, duplicate hit, destroyed target, disabled adapter, 2D-only API scan, and domain-authority tests.
Manual/playable: inspect synthetic mount rays/hits in an isolated fixture and confirm domain results remain authoritative.
tasks.handoff_notes: WP tasks supply concrete package-owned adapters/prefabs behind this boundary without editing it.

tasks.title: Expose read-only four-mount HUD status
tasks.summary: Project immutable four-slot combat state through the accepted HUD contract without Unity or HUD visuals.
tasks.objective: Expose stable slot order, readiness, firing phase, heat/charge, recovery, power level, normal/empowered/fallback mode, and mount fault state to later presentation work.
tasks.context: S1.2 frozen question: can one shared aim/fire intent drive four independently simulated mounts with unlimited normal fire, independent power banks, bounded movement influence, readable state, and modular behavior inside the accepted ten-day cap?
inputs: Consumed contract: Four-Mount Weapons/HUD v1 from CS-005.
Immutable mount, power, and coordinator state from CB-002/CB-003/CB-006.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: accepted planning/requirements, UnityAdapters, UI/HUD prefabs or scenes, movement, ContentPackages, registries, mission state, and saves.
Serialized ownership: none. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none.
The projector is plain C# application code and read-only; it cannot mutate combat state or infer presentation effects.
Rollback: remove the three owned files; combat simulation remains unchanged. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: Exactly four stable ordered slots expose readiness, phase, heat/charge, recovery, power level, mode/fallback, and fault state.
Projection is deterministic, immutable, Unity-free, and cannot mutate source state.
Missing or invalid slot data fails visibly rather than silently reordering or fabricating state.
verification: Automated: mixed state, stable order, fallback mode, fault state, immutability, missing slot, and repeated projection tests.
Manual/playable: human readability review of textual four-slot snapshots confirms state distinctions are understandable without color alone.
non_goals: no UI Toolkit or uGUI assets
tasks.handoff_notes: Later UI tasks consume this snapshot and may not read mutable combat state directly.

tasks.title: Add four-mount combat-foundation evidence scenarios
tasks.summary: Exercise the complete combat foundation with frozen synthetic profiles in the existing benchmark arena.
tasks.objective: Verify shared aim, mixed cadence, simultaneous fire, mixed readiness, independent power fallback, fault isolation, bounded movement influence, input lifecycle, restart, diagnostics, performance, and evidence identity without claiming weapon-package completion.
tasks.context: S1.2 frozen question: can one shared aim/fire intent drive four independently simulated mounts with unlimited normal fire, independent power banks, bounded movement influence, readable state, and modular behavior inside the accepted ten-day cap?
inputs: Evidence configuration, diagnostics, benchmark arena, lifecycle, performance, manifests, smoke, and review protocol from EH-002/003/004/006/007/008/009/010.
Movement runtime/status/evidence from MT-010/011/012 and complete combat foundation from CB-006 through CB-010.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: accepted planning/requirements, EH-004 benchmark scene, movement-owned files, ContentPackages/Weapons, generated registries, scenes, prefabs, ScriptableObjects, HUD visuals, concrete weapons, enemies, mission state, saves, and rewards.
Serialized ownership: exclusive ownership of four-mount-combat-foundation-profile-v1.json only; the evidence scene is read-only. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none.
Keep this integration-only: reuse existing EH infrastructure and synthetic profiles; do not add new evidence frameworks, weapon content, audiovisual content, or benchmark-scene edits.

Rollback: remove the three owned files; lower-level evidence and combat tests remain valid. Prototype debt: synthetic profiles are evidence-only and expire when representative WP package evidence replaces them.
acceptance_criteria: The frozen session demonstrates one shared aim/fire stream driving exactly four mounts with different cadence and simultaneous mixed readiness.
Per-mount power depletion falls back independently, one injected mount fault leaves the other three operational, and combat movement influence remains bounded.
Focus loss clears held combat input, rapid restart returns to the same frozen initial state, diagnostics classify invalid sessions, and evidence identity/manifests remain reproducible.
The scenario explicitly states that five amended Stage 1 weapon packages, final audiovisual identity, HUD visuals, and encounter balance are not complete.
verification: Automated: deterministic replay, shared aim, mixed cadence/readiness/power, fault isolation, recoil cap, focus-loss, restart, diagnostics, manifest, and performance-budget scenario tests.

Manual/playable: human lead reviews shared aiming, four independent firing traces, recoil/movement interaction, color-independent status readability, rapid restart, and injected fault isolation; record pass/fail notes and required bounded iteration.
Manual/playable dependency: WP package implementation may not claim combat-foundation acceptance until this human review is recorded in the evidence package.
proof_required: valid and intentionally invalid manifested evidence packages
human playable review record covering aim, independence, recoil, readability, restart, and fault isolation
edge_cases: one mount faults during power fire
tasks.handoff_notes: WP tasks consume this accepted foundation and replace synthetic profiles with package-owned evidence without editing EH-004.
tasks.notes: Milestone-cap spend: 0.65 focused lead day. Batch direct spend totals 4.50 focused lead days, leaving 5.50 of the S1.2 ten-day cap for the amended five-weapon set, HUD, temporary audiovisual/readability work, human review, and bounded iteration.
